mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20d forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 0.3 percentage point increase over 3 months, markets price in a deteriorating labor market and rising recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining HOUST and rising FEDFUNDS signals tightening credit conditions choking housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumption slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone carries this predictive power; the interaction of falling housing starts under a rising-rate regime is required.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls >5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing slowdown
failure_condition: Joint HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise fails to produce negative median 20d forward returns relative to unconditional baseline, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over univariate HOUST model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating INDPRO growth signals capacity utilization peaking and impending earnings deceleration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship reverses or weakens in early-cycle recovery regimes where INDPRO deceleration reflects normalization rather than contraction.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth deceleration signal fails to precede below-median 60d SPX returns in late-cycle subsample, or the sign flips inconsistently across two consecutive late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in PAYEMS growth reflects strengthening labor income and consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to payroll print surprises.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month acceleration shows Rank IC below 0.03 with 5d forward returns over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 3.5%, markets anticipate more aggressive future FEDFUNDS tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation episodes show positive median 20d SPX returns, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in RSAFS reflects robust consumer spending momentum, a direct driver of corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following the retail sales release.
failure_condition: RSAFS momentum signal produces Rank IC below 0.03 with 5d forward SPX returns over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery regime, simultaneous acceleration in PAYEMS growth and decline in UNRATE jointly confirm a durable labor market recovery that supports sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in late-cycle regimes this same joint pattern instead signals labor market overheating and wage-cost pressure, reversing the expected sign.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5% and UNRATE falls over same period
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS acceleration and UNRATE decline fails to produce above-median 60d SPX returns in early-cycle subsample, or sign is inconsistent with regime hypothesis across at least two recovery episodes.
